notes: Solid, complete Pong implementation with proper ball physics, CPU AI, scoring, serve logic, pause, and audio; minor issue is the ball scoring check uses ball.x < -ball.w which could allow slight clipping past the left edge before registering, but overall nearly flawless.
A flawless, highly polished implementation of Pong with excellent game feel, proper AI, sound effects, and perfect retro aesthetics.

A solid and mostly complete Pong clone with proper scoring, AI, pause, serve, and game-over flow, but it lacks two-player support and has minor control/input quirks that keep it from feeling flawless.
